core_concepts: Customizable review rules and prompt templates
supported_claims: Open-sourced by Alibaba in 2024, the tool uses large language models to automatically review pull requests and provide feedback.
Supports multiple LLM backends including OpenAI, Tongyi Qianwen, and local models via Ollama.
Review rules can be defined in YAML configuration files, allowing teams to enforce coding standards and best practices.
common_pitfalls: Over-reliance on AI reviews without human oversight can miss context-specific issues.
Misconfiguration of webhook secrets may lead to unauthorized access or failed integrations.
Using overly complex prompt templates can cause inconsistent or irrelevant feedback.
research_questions: How does open-code-review compare to other AI code review tools like CodeRabbit or Amazon CodeGuru Reviewer?
What are the performance and cost implications of using different LLM backends (e.g., OpenAI vs. local models)?
How does the tool handle large diffs or multi-file changes in terms of token limits and review quality?
success_criteria: Successfully install and configure the tool on a test repository with at least one automated review triggered.
Customize a review rule (e.g., enforce naming conventions) and verify the AI provides relevant feedback.
Measure reduction in manual review time or increase in code quality metrics after adopting the tool.
